articles.title: Prediction market Kalshi takes legal stand against Arizona’s gambling laws
articles.summary: Kalshi is challenging Arizona's gambling laws in court, presumably to defend its prediction market operations in the state. If successful, the case could set important legal precedent for how prediction markets are regulated nationwide, potentially opening doors in other restrictive jurisdictions.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Called Most Oscar Winners as Trading Tops $105 Million
articles.summary: Prediction markets accurately forecasted the majority of Oscar winners this year while generating over $105 million in trading volume across the ceremony. The strong predictive performance at such high volume proves these markets can handle major entertainment events with both accuracy and serious liquidity, attracting real money from confident traders.

articles.title: Polymarket and Kalshi Got the Oscars About 80 Percent Right
articles.summary: Polymarket and Kalshi correctly predicted roughly 80% of Oscar winners, marking another solid performance for prediction markets in entertainment betting. The results add more evidence that crowd wisdom beats expert punditry in awards shows, potentially drawing more mainstream attention and liquidity to political and financial prediction markets.

articles.title: Gambling on everything
articles.summary: The betting industry is expanding beyond traditional sports into every conceivable category, from weather patterns and celebrity breakups to political outcomes and economic indicators. This trend validates what prediction markets have always known: people want to bet on everything that matters, and smart money follows wherever genuine information asymmetries exist.

articles.title: Kalshi and Polymarket correctly predicted 19 out of 24 Oscar winners. Here are the ones they missed.
articles.summary: Prediction markets on Kalshi and Polymarket nailed 79% of Oscar winners this year, missing only 5 categories out of 24 total awards. The strong performance adds more credibility to betting markets as entertainment forecasting tools, especially since Oscar predictions rely heavily on industry insider knowledge and campaign momentum rather than pure statistical analysis.

articles.title: Prediction markets watch
articles.summary: Prediction markets are gaining mainstream attention as news outlets and analysts start tracking their signals more seriously. This legitimizes betting markets as information sources and could drive more liquidity from institutions who previously dismissed them as gambling.

articles.title: Gamblers trying to win a bet on Polymarket are vowing to kill me if I don’t rewrite an Iran missile story
articles.summary: Bettors on Polymarket are apparently threatening a journalist with death over how they covered an Iran missile story, presumably because the reporting could affect the outcome of their wagers. This highlights how prediction markets can create perverse incentives where traders have financial stakes in how news gets reported, potentially pressuring journalists to frame stories in ways that favor certain betting positions.

articles.title: Wall Street Is Already Betting on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Wall Street firms are placing bets on prediction market platforms themselves, treating these forecasting venues as investment opportunities rather than just trading tools. This institutional money validates prediction markets as a legitimate asset class and will likely fuel rapid expansion, better liquidity, and higher-stakes political and economic betting.

articles.title: Traders Flip Senate Control Bet as Democrats Overtake Republicans on Kalshi, Polymarket
articles.summary: Prediction market traders are now betting Democrats will control the Senate after the 2024 elections, with the party overtaking Republicans as favorites on both Kalshi and Polymarket. The flip signals either late-breaking campaign momentum or smart money recognizing value in previously underpriced Democratic odds.

articles.title: Polymarket Five-Minute Bets Capture ‘Addictive’ Crypto Craze
articles.summary: Polymarket launched five-minute betting windows that are hooking crypto traders with rapid-fire prediction fixes. The move signals how prediction markets are morphing into gambling-adjacent products that prioritize user engagement over forecasting accuracy.

articles.title: AI agents are quietly rewriting prediction market trading
articles.summary: Autonomous AI systems are now placing bets and executing trades across prediction markets without human oversight, fundamentally changing how these platforms operate. This shift means human traders are increasingly competing against algorithms that can process information faster, trade around the clock, and potentially manipulate prices in ways that weren't possible before.

articles.title: Mojtaba Khamenei To Be Removed Before 2026 Ends? Here's What Crypto Prediction Market Says About Iran's New Supreme Leader
articles.summary: Crypto prediction markets are betting on whether Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's newly appointed Supreme Leader, will get ousted before 2026 wraps up. The action suggests traders think Iran's power transition won't be smooth, creating volatile odds on Middle East geopolitical events that smart money can capitalize on.
